name: Fabric (SSH Deployment & Remote Execution)

auth.notes: SSH key-based (recommended) or password auth. Leverages Paramiko under the hood

performance.notes: Network-bound; SSH connection overhead + remote command execution time

agent_readiness.notes: SSH-based deployment and remote command execution. from fabric import Connection. c = Connection('web1.example.com'). result = c.run('uname -s'). c.put('local.tar.gz', '/tmp/'). c.get('/var/log/app.log', 'local.log'). c.sudo('systemctl restart nginx'). c.cd('/var/www'): c.run('git pull'). fabfile.py: from fabric import task. @task. def deploy(c): c.run('git pull'). fab -H web1.example.com deploy. Group: from fabric import SerialGroup, ThreadedGroup. g = SerialGroup('web1', 'web2'). g.run('whoami'). Inline SSH: Connection('user@host:port', connect_kwargs={'key_filename': '/path/to/key'}).

gotchas: Fabric 3 (fabric3) is a different package from Fabric 1/2 — don't confuse PyPI packages
No built-in idempotency — must write idempotent shell commands yourself
sudo() requires passwordless sudo or TTY allocation (pty=True)
No type annotations — limited IDE support
Connection pooling not automatic — manage context managers carefully
best_when: SSH-based deployment automation (git pull, restart services)
Remote server administration with Python scripting
Multi-host deployment with SerialGroup or ThreadedGroup
File transfer (put/get) combined with remote command execution
avoid_when: Complex multi-host orchestration (use Ansible for idempotency)
Large-scale infrastructure management (use Terraform + cloud CLIs)
summary: Fabric simplifies SSH-based deployment and remote command execution. Connection('host').run('cmd'), .put(), .get(), .sudo(). fabfile.py + @task decorator. SerialGroup/ThreadedGroup for multi-host. BSD-2 by Jeff Forcier. Built on Paramiko. Best for Python-driven SSH deployments. No idempotency — use Ansible for that. Fabric 3 is a different PyPI package from Fabric 1/2. No type annotations.
